name: Escales and Sant Antoni Reservoirs Loop
name_original: Los dos embalses: de Escaules hasta Sant Antoni - circular desde Tremp
description: This difficult 70.1-mile (112.7 km) road cycling route from Tremp explores the scenic Escales and Sant Antoni reservoirs.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Tremp traverse a diverse landscape in Catalonia, Spain, characterized by significant elevation changes and varied terrain. The region features prominent reservoirs like Pantà de Sant Antoni and the Noguera Pallaresa, alongside the Montsec mountain range, which includes sectors like Estall, Ares, and Rubies. Cyclists encounter a mix of rocky, wild areas and pre-mountainous agricultural settings, often with routes leading through semi-arid mountainous landscapes. Best road cycling routes around Tremp The most popular road cycling route is Escales and Sant Antoni Reservoirs Loop, a 70.0 miles (112.7 km) trail that takes 6 hours 2 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant climbs and descents around the reservoirs. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Coll de Montllobar (1080 m) loop from Estació de Tremp, a difficult 50.9 miles (81.9 km) path. This route leads through a semi-arid mountainous landscape, peaking at Coll de Montllobar. Local road cyclists also love the Coll de Comiols – Benavent de la Conca loop from Estació de Tremp, a 50.8 miles (81.7 km) trail leading through pre-mountainous agricultural settings, often completed in about 4 hours 10 minutes. Road cycling around Tremp is defined by its reservoirs, the Montsec mountain range, and varied terrain from rocky areas to agricultural settings. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Tremp are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Tremp's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Tremp?
A: There are nearly 20 road cycling routes around Tremp, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes cater to various fitness levels, with a mix of moderate and difficult options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Tremp?
A: Road cycling routes around Tremp traverse a diverse landscape in Catalonia, Spain. You can expect significant elevation changes and varied terrain, from rocky, wild areas to pre-mountainous agricultural settings. Many routes feature prominent reservoirs like Pantà de Sant Antoni and the Noguera Pallaresa, and explore sectors of the Montsec mountain range, often leading through semi-arid mountainous landscapes.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Tremp?
A: Yes, Tremp is well-suited for experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge. About half of the routes in the area are classified as difficult, featuring significant climbs and descents. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943328552/escales-and-sant-antoni-reservoirs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escales and Sant Antoni Reservoirs Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 70-mile ride with over 2,100 meters of elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Montllobar (1080 m) loop from Estació de Tremp&lt;/a&gt;, which peaks at Coll de Montllobar through a semi-arid mountainous landscape.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or landmarks I can see along the road cycling routes in Tremp?
A: Many routes offer breathtaking views and pass by notable natural features. You can enjoy stunning vistas of the Escales and Sant Antoni Reservoirs, with specific viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3952361" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Escales Reservoir and the stone bridge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5145330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sant Antoni Reservoir and Dam&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the spectacular Montsec mountain range, offering rugged and picturesque backdrops for your rides. Some paths even connect old villages, providing glimpses of traditional architecture and culture.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Tremp?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Tremp are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943328552/escales-and-sant-antoni-reservoirs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escales and Sant Antoni Reservoirs Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Montllobar (1080 m) loop from Estació de Tremp&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Comiols – Benavent de la Conca loop from Estació de Tremp&lt;/a&gt;. These circular routes provide a convenient way to explore the diverse landscapes of the region.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Tremp?
A: The road cycling routes in Tremp are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from challenging mountain passes to scenic reservoir loops, and the opportunity to explore the diverse landscapes of Catalonia. The significant elevation changes and the natural beauty of the Montsec mountain range and reservoirs are frequently highlighted as key attractions.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Tremp?
A: Tremp offers good road cycling conditions for much of the year due to its Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for longer rides and challenging climbs. While summer can be warm, early morning rides are still enjoyable. Winter cycling is also possible, though higher elevation routes might be colder.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Tremp?
A: While many routes around Tremp feature significant climbs and descents, making them more suitable for experienced riders, there are areas with less demanding terrain. For family-friendly options, consider exploring routes around the reservoirs or in the pre-mountainous agricultural settings, which may offer flatter sections. Always check the route details for elevation and difficulty before heading out with family.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Tremp?
A: Many road cycling routes around Tremp originate from the municipality itself, making it a convenient starting point. You can typically find parking in and around the town of Tremp. For routes starting outside the main town, look for designated parking areas near trailheads or local villages. Always check local signage for parking regulations.

Q: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation for cyclists in Tremp?
A: Tremp, as a municipality, offers various amenities for visitors, including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. These can serve as convenient stops for refreshments or overnight stays, especially for those undertaking multi-day tours. The Epicentre in Tremp also serves as a visitor center for the UNESCO World Geopark Orígens, providing local information.

Q: What is the longest road cycling route available in Tremp?
A: The longest road cycling route listed in the area is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31530523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alt de Fontllonga – Congost de Vilanova loop from Estació de Tremp&lt;/a&gt;, which spans nearly 120 kilometers (74.5 miles) and involves over 2,000 meters of elevation gain, making it a challenging full-day ride.

Q: Are there any less crowded road cycling routes in Tremp?
A: While Tremp is gaining popularity, many routes, especially those venturing into the Montsec mountain range or through more remote agricultural areas, offer a sense of solitude. Exploring routes that are not the 'most popular' can lead to quieter experiences. The diverse network of nearly 20 routes provides ample opportunity to find less crowded paths, particularly during off-peak seasons or weekdays.

The Escales swamp is a reservoir that belongs to the Noguera Ribagorçana river, created by a dam. The road that winds it has no exits and separates the two autonomous communities of Aragon and Catalonia.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging route, and it typically takes around 6 hours to complete the full circular ride. This estimate is for active cycling time and doesn't include stops for rest or sightseeing.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' for racebike cycling. It involves significant climbs, including the Coll de Montllobar (1080 m) and Coll de la Creu de Pervés, and requires a good level of fitness and experience with road cycling.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular route from Tremp?
faqs.answer: Tremp serves as an excellent starting point for this circular route. You'll find various parking options within the town. Tremp is also known for being cycling-friendly, with some hotels offering bike storage and maintenance facilities.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily on paved roads, offering a scenic road cycling experience through the Pre-Pyrenees mountains of Catalonia. While mostly paved, be prepared for varying road conditions, as some older sections, particularly along the reservoir, might be worn. You'll encounter diverse terrain including coves, cliffs, forests, and rural farmlands, with significant elevation changes.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy ever-changing vistas, especially around the Sant Antoni reservoir. Key highlights include the Escales Reservoir, the challenging climbs of Coll de Montllobar (1080 m) and Coll de la Creu de Pervés, and the Scenic Road to La Pobla de Segur. The route offers panoramic views of the tranquil reservoirs, the Noguera Pallaresa River, and the dramatic Pre-Pyrenees mountains, with autumnal colors enhancing the scenery in fall.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed in various seasons, but late October has been noted for its mild temperatures, allowing for comfortable cycling, and the beautiful autumnal colors. The region's climate generally provides a pleasant backdrop for cycling, but always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly for cyclists?
faqs.answer: As this is a challenging road cycling route, bringing a dog along, especially if it requires being carried or is not accustomed to long distances alongside a bike, might be difficult. There are no specific regulations prohibiting dogs, but consider the length, difficulty, and road conditions for your pet's safety and comfort.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and passes through natural areas. However, as parts of the route are within the Orígens UNESCO Global Geopark, it's important to respect local regulations regarding nature conservation.

faqs.question: Does this route pass through the Orígens UNESCO Global Geopark, and are there any special regulations?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route traverses parts of the Orígens UNESCO Global Geopark. While there are no specific fees for cycling, visitors are expected to adhere to general geopark guidelines, which include respecting the natural environment, staying on marked paths where applicable, and not disturbing wildlife or geological formations. Always practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food, drink, or accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, Tremp is a significant cycling destination and offers various amenities. You'll find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in Tremp. The route also passes near La Pobla de Segur, which provides additional services for cyclists.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares segments with several other official trails. You'll find yourself on sections of trails such as [CIMA LL06] Creu de Perves * Pont de Suert, Serrat de Sant Roc, Ruta dels Llacs, and Rocs de Queralt-Collegats-Gramuntill, among others. This offers opportunities to explore extended routes if you wish.

waypoints.name: Coll de la Creu de Pervés
